sections.paragraph: to melt away, to melt, hence to pine, to languish. (The signification of melting or pining is widely extended amongst cognate verbs, as אָדַב, דָּבָא, דּוּב, דָּוָה, Syr. ܕܘܽܒ, Arab. ذَأَبَ, ذَابَ, and the idea is variously applied either to the languor of a sick or old person, or to fear. The primary idea is that of melting with heat, zerfchmelzen, zer=fliefßen, comp. זוּב. Amongst the Indo-Germanic languages this may be compared with Pers. تاب heat, تافتن, تفتن to warm, to kindle, = θάπτω, تباهيدن to melt away; Sansc. tapa, Lat. tepeo. Kindred is τήκω, דָּאַנ.) It is applied to the eye, pining away with grief, Psa 88:10 (see עָשַׁשׁ , בָּלָה ); to the soul (נֶפֶשׁ), Jer 31:25 to the person himself, Jer 31:12. Hence-
